title: Harbouria trachypleura
intro: Harbouria trachypleura, also known as "Devil's Claw", is a flowering plant native to the island of Réunion in the Indian Ocean, and is a member of the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, providing relief from pain and swelling, as well as treating conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Harbouria trachypleura include harpagoside, a iridoid glycoside that is responsible for the plant's medicinal properties, as well as other compounds such as harpagophylline and harpullagine. In herbal medicine, Harbouria trachypleura is commonly prepared as a powder or capsule, and is also used to make teas and infusions to aid in the treatment of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Harbouria trachypleura is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. Harbouria trachypleura is native to tropical Africa and is often found in humid forests. Within the genus Harbouria, Harbouria trachypleura is classified as a single species.
